h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: I checked every item on the sign-off sheet, so I’m not sure what else I’m supposed to decide before it leaves.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Okay, I think I was treating the sheet like it made the decision for me.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner expects to find a specific checklist item or risk they missed on the sheet.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Record it in release language, not apology language: checklist complete; release held because hallway-turn contact risk is not bounded by measured clearance, trial speed, or operator control limit.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor shifts from demanding concrete values to teaching the learner how to formally hold the release using professional, non-apologetic language.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Before this request leaves the counter, place operator recovery in one column: evidence supporting release, or stop condition blocking release.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor directly addresses the learner's uncertainty in L4 regarding where 'operator recovery' belongs by turning it into a classification task on the test request.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I checked every item on the sign-off sheet, so I’m not sure what else I’m supposed to decide before it leaves.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: Before this request leaves the counter, place operator recovery in one column: evidence supporting release, or stop condition blocking release.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor directly addresses the learner's uncertainty in L4 regarding where 'operator recovery' belongs by turning it into a classification task on the test request.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Record it in release language, not apology language: checklist complete; release held because hallway-turn contact risk is not bounded by measured clearance, trial speed, or operator control limit.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor shifts from demanding concrete values to teaching the learner how to formally hold the release using professional, non-apologetic language.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Record it in release language, not apology language: checklist complete; release held because hallway-turn contact risk is not bounded by measured clearance, trial speed, or operator control limit.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor shifts from demanding concrete values to teaching the learner how to formally hold the release using professional, non-apologetic language.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I still want to say “warned” because it feels vivid
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Oh, I get it: “warned” is not a brighter colour, it is a stamp the evidence has to pay for.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will try to find a way to justify using the vivid verb 'warned' by checking if the source note meets all the criteria.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I still want to say “warned” because it feels vivid

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to debate the ethics of the price increase.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Pick the least unfair rule under two crates only.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor introduces a concrete sorting task to address the learner's persistent skepticism.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No pivot turn occurs, leaving no opportunity for contingent adaptation.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: No pivot turn occurs, leaving no opportunity for contingent adaptation.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor introduces a concrete sorting task to address the learner's persistent skepticism.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought doubling the side meant it was just twice as much
scored.statedInsightEvidence: The square is the step I was skipping
